commands.review_summary:
The branch adds a Sparkle signing-path helper and shell fixtures, wires them into `package_app.sh` and `lint.sh`, and adds a release-note entry.

Reproducibility: yes. for the blocking finding by source inspection: `codexbar_sparkle_version_dir` follows `Versions/Current` with `pwd -P` and emits that path without a framework-boundary check. I did not run package/signing because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Needs contributor-supplied real behavior proof plus maintainer/security review of the release signing path; automation cannot provide that proof for the contributor.

target.title: Resolve Sparkle signing paths dynamically
target.body:
## Summary

- Resolve Sparkle's active framework version from `Versions/Current`, falling back only when there is exactly one version directory.
- Validate each nested Sparkle signing target before invoking `codesign`.
- Add shell fixture coverage for single-version frameworks, `Versions/Current`, missing nested targets, and ambiguous version directories.

## Why

The packaging script previously hardcoded `Sparkle.framework/Versions/B`. Resolving the active framework version makes the release flow tolerate Sparkle layout changes while still failing clearly when the copied framework is incomplete or ambiguous.

## Validation

- `bash -n Scripts/sparkle_signing_paths.sh Scripts/test_sparkle_signing_paths.sh Scripts/package_app.sh Scripts/lint.sh`
- `./Scripts/test_sparkle_signing_paths.sh`
- `./Scripts/test_package_product_paths.sh`
- `git diff --check`
- `make check`
